text: Umrah and Hajj: key health messages before, during and after your trip WHO and Libya’s Ministry of Health Sign Strategic Collaboration Framework to Strengthen the National Health System WHO and IsDB strengthen lifesaving care for women and children in Afghanistan Patients in Homs receive quality care when it matters most Navigating remote Sindh to deliver life-saving vaccines in Pakistan The plan focuses on six regional priorities.
source: WHO Regional Office for the Eastern Mediterranean

text: Five are taken from GPW 14: three technical pillars – Promote, Provide and Protect – plus two enabling pillars, Power and Perform. Polio has been added as a sixth priority, as the Eastern Mediterranean is the only region where it remains endemic. The World Health Organization (WHO) is the directing and coordinating authority for public health within the United Nations system.
source: WHO Regional Office for the Eastern Mediterranean

text: The WHO Regional Office for the Eastern Mediterranean is one of WHO’s 6 regional offices around the world. It serves the WHO Eastern Mediterranean Region, which comprises 21 Member States and occupied Palestinian territory (including East Jerusalem), with a population of nearly 745 million people.
source: WHO Regional Office for the Eastern Mediterranean
